title: brother's spirit in Iolanthe spirit contact in Port Phillip Bay
publication_or_organisation: Sherlock Holmes and spiritualism: Sir Arthur Conan Doyle in Victoria; State Library Victoria
australian_relation: State Library Victoria public article describing a Victorian spiritualist account linked to Port Phillip Bay.
coordinate_evidence_note: State Library Victoria page says the brothers went out on the boat Iolanthe into Port Phillip Bay and describes a medium's spirit contact account; coordinates use the state-checked OSM/Nominatim Port Phillip Bay named-feature result.
evidence_summary: State Library Victoria recounts that in 1883 two brothers went out on the boat Iolanthe into Port Phillip Bay and never returned. Their spiritualist parents employed the medium George Spriggs, who reportedly made contact with a brother's spirit and was told that the boat had capsized, but that the brothers were now at peace.
